what_it_does: Scans a directory tree of source files, sends each file's text to a local LLM chat endpoint with a strict real-vs-stub classification prompt, and writes the resulting structured records to a JSONL file with resume and threaded execution.
observations: Uses a hardcoded local OpenAI-compatible endpoint at http://localhost:8000/v1/chat completions with model cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit.
System prompt asks the model to judge whether each file is real-working, partial, stub, or scaffold and to return strict JSON with what, real_or_stub, implements, connects_to, and notable.
Reads only the first 60,000 characters of each file and skips files shorter than 20 characters.
Finds files via a bash subprocess using find and grep, excluding node_modules, .git, dist, build, _system/snapshots, and .obsidian, and matching .ts, .tsx, .py, .js, .mjs, and .sql.
Maintains an incremental resume list: loads existing output, keeps only successful records, marks them done, and reprocesses only remaining files.
Retries each LLM call up to four times, using response_format json_object only on the first two attempts.
Processes files concurrently with a ThreadPoolExecutor of six workers and appends JSONL lines incrementally.
standouts: Incremental resume and deduplication against prior output.
Guided JSON mode fallback to unguided retries.
conventions: Top-level script with hardcoded constants and inline execution.
Uses standard library only (urllib, subprocess, concurrent.futures).
JSONL append output with per-line flushing.
concerns: Hardcoded Windows/WSL-style ROOT path and localhost endpoint make the script environment-specific.
Subprocess shell command quotes ROOT but could still break on embedded single quotes or special characters.
Reads only first 60k characters, potentially missing important file content.
No authentication or API key for the endpoint.
No schema validation of the LLM response beyond JSON parsing.
Concurrent writes to the same output file object from multiple threads may interleave lines.
errors='ignore' when reading files can silently corrupt non-UTF8 content.
notes: A one-off Python scanner that feeds source files to a local LLM and records structured real-vs-stub assessments in a JSONL file, with resume and threaded execution.
questions: What is the intended downstream consumer of the generated JSONL?
Why is guided JSON mode used only on the first two attempts?
Why is the file read limit set to exactly 60,000 characters?
How is the local model endpoint expected to be launched?
What does 'wizard-run-1' signify in the project path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to read one source file and judge real vs stub with strict JSON; the file actually orchestrates scanning many files, resuming, retrying, and persisting results, so it does what it says plus batch orchestration.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a structured audit trail of which files in a messy, possibly AI-generated project are actually implemented versus placeholder, likely to inform a migration or cleanup effort.
novelty: Uses a small local AWQ chat model to classify code completeness file-by-file and persists the judgments in a resumable JSONL format.
contribution: A reusable pattern for LLM-driven file-level code audits: the real/stub classification schema, the resume/deduplication loop, and the threaded orchestration that sends file contents to a local endpoint.
summary_for_embedding: A Python script that scans a directory tree of source files, sends each file's contents to a local chat-completions endpoint with a strict real-vs-stub classification prompt, and appends the resulting JSONL records to an output file with incremental resume and threaded processing.
intention_for_embedding: To automatically inventory a scattered AI-generated codebase by labeling each source file as real, partial, stub, or scaffold.

symbols.does: Sends a chat-completion request to a local LLM endpoint and returns the model's message content.
symbols.description: Builds a JSON payload using the hardcoded system prompt, the provided user content (file path and text), and an optional response_format when guided is true, then posts it to URL with urllib and returns the string content of the first choice.

symbols.does: Reads a single source file and attempts to obtain a structured LLM assessment, with retries.
symbols.description: Reads up to 60,000 characters from the given path, returns a stub record for very short files, and otherwise retries up to four times to parse the LLM response into the required schema, using guided JSON mode on the first two attempts and injecting the relative path into the returned record.
